ProjectsScreen.title: Projects

paragraphs: A selection of shipped products and websites, not an exhaustive inventory.
Each project is included to demonstrate product reasoning, clear architecture, or high-standard frontend execution.

ProjectsScreen.selectionNote: Curation is part of perceived level: quality, intent, and coherence over volume.

meta.title: Projects
meta.description: Selected case studies and shipped work - Estime Vangu.

fleetscan.name: FleetScan
fleetscan.tagline: Fleet incident reporting through QR and plate scan
fleetscan.summary: A mobile-first field tool to identify a truck and report fleet status in seconds: damage, compliance, and manager visibility.
highlights: Supabase authentication with admin / manager / driver role model
Camera QR scan and normalized license plate entry
PostgreSQL RLS and strict Zod validation at data boundaries

shadow.name: Shadow
shadow.tagline: Private messaging, shared calendar, and installable app
shadow.summary: An installable app for a restricted group: disappearing messages, group threads, events, and notifications.
highlights: Supabase Realtime with SWR cache strategy
Complete PWA setup with manifest and service worker
Architecture, security, and pre-deployment audit documentation

jikowood.name: Jikowood
jikowood.tagline: Premium showcase redesign for a high-end carpenter
jikowood.summary: An immersive digital experience for premium craftsmanship: fullscreen gallery, smooth scroll, and visual-first narrative.
highlights: Gallery with pure-function previous / next navigation
GSAP + Lenis with reduced-motion support
Vitest, Playwright E2E, and documented engineering conventions

portfolio-pro.name: Portfolio Pro
portfolio-pro.tagline: Product engineer portfolio with a narrative shell
portfolio-pro.summary: This website itself: frontend-first identity, modular GSAP motion architecture, i18n without URL prefix, and tokenized styling.
highlights: Cinematic splash and coordinated route transitions
Theme interpolation across 14 CSS variables through GSAP
Product strategy documented in phases before content production

enna.tagline: HR consulting - talent, diversity, employer brand

GSAP splash and Framer Motion modal interactions

maxweljones.name: Maxwel Jones
maxweljones.summary: An institutional one-page website with consultant network presentation, Web3Forms contact flow, and booking UI already prepared.

Network slide and member directory experience

folio-photo.name: PhotoVibes

purpose-sport.summary: A premium sports agency website presenting twelve athletes with profiles, stats, embedded videos, and a direct contact flow.

react-player integration and Vercel Analytics tracking

fleetscan.name: FleetScan
fleetscan.tagline: Fleet incidents, field scan, manager oversight
fleetscan.role: Product design, frontend architecture, Supabase integration, authentication, and scan workflow ownership.

meta.title: FleetScan - Case study
meta.description: Fleet incident operations app with QR flow, role model, RLS, and strict validation.

paragraphs: Field teams need to report a truck status in seconds, without friction and without unstable manual entry.
Managers need a reliable fleet view without heavy enterprise tooling or fragile shared spreadsheets.
Mobile-first workflow: scan by QR or plate, open the vehicle sheet, and make a binary report (damage vs nothing to report).
Lean Supabase backend: SSR auth, role-scoped RLS, versioned SQL migrations, and strict server-side validation.

context.title: Product context
paragraphs: FleetScan (scratch_tracker repository) addresses a real depot workflow where speed and clarity matter more than feature volume.
The MVP covers the driver journey plus a staff overview, while the roadmap openly tracks photos, Realtime, and stronger cache strategy.
bullets: Concrete field constraints shape every UX decision
Scope is deliberate to avoid fake completeness
Roadmap remains technical and product-aligned

architecture.title: Architecture
paragraphs: Routing is organized with `(splash)`, `(public)`, and `(protected)` groups to keep responsibilities explicit.
A feature / controller / view structure drives scan flows, truck dashboard behavior, and fleet overview composition.
bullets: lib/ includes Zod schemas, server actions, and plate/date mappers
Supabase SSR session middleware handles authenticated boundaries
Around 30 Vitest tests cover critical business logic

decisions.title: Key decisions
paragraphs: fr / nl / en localization is cookie-based to keep URLs stable for multi-country field usage.
Zod at the boundaries enforces a strict rule: external data is never trusted by default.
bullets: No over-abstraction: one function per file and isolated features
Camera scanning is isolated in its own dedicated module
Shared RLS rules are versioned and documented in SQL migrations

execution.title: Execution and next steps
paragraphs: The foundation is production-oriented: tests are in place, product documentation exists, and the structure scales without rewrite pressure.
Next steps are explicit: field captures, final create flow completion, and Vercel deployment with monitoring.
bullets: Core architecture is mature enough for hardening
Last-mile delivery is identified, not hidden
Monitoring is planned as part of shipping, not afterthought

shadow.name: Shadow
shadow.tagline: Private communication and planning for a closed circle
shadow.role: Fullstack implementation on Next.js and Supabase: messaging, calendar workflows, PWA setup, and security hardening.

meta.title: Shadow - Case study
meta.description: Private messaging and shared calendar app with Realtime, PWA delivery, RLS, and E2E tests.

paragraphs: A restricted group needs a private communication space with disappearing messages and shared planning, without depending on public consumer tools.
The experience must remain smooth on mobile, install like a native app, and keep technical behavior predictable.
The stack combines Next.js and Supabase with SSR auth, modular CRUD per domain entity, and Realtime updates for threads and badges.
PWA support is production-configured through manifest, service worker, and explicit middleware exclusions to avoid deployment surprises.

context.title: Context
paragraphs: Shadow combines 24-hour ephemeral messaging, direct and group conversations, and an events calendar in one coherent private product.
Fourteen SQL migrations version the data model, and architecture/security documents include pre-deployment audit findings.
bullets: Product scope is coherent, not feature-stacked
Data model evolution is tracked through migrations
Audit output directly informs implementation priorities

architecture.title: Architecture
paragraphs: The codebase is split by feature domains (auth, messages, calendar, home) to preserve ownership clarity.
Entity-level `lib/supabase/CRUD/` modules maintain readability and make integration testing straightforward.
bullets: SWR provides responsive caching behavior for message flows
Supabase Storage handles media payloads
Middleware combines getClaims() checks and auth rate limiting

decisions.title: Decisions
paragraphs: RLS is treated as the primary security boundary, with known pitfalls documented transparently, including conversation access edge cases.
Framer Motion interactions are wrapped with reduced-motion handling for sensitive or high-frequency UX moments.
bullets: Explicit webpack settings ensure PWA and Vercel compatibility
Bundle analysis is integrated to keep performance under control
Five Playwright specs currently secure the auth-critical surface

execution.title: Execution
paragraphs: The MVP is advanced and credible, with final hardening still required (notably CSP and error boundary reinforcement).
It demonstrates a complete technical product lifecycle, not only visual frontend output.
bullets: Core product behavior is stable in real conditions
Remaining security items are known and actionable
Delivery narrative stays honest about what is left

jikowood.name: Jikowood
jikowood.tagline: High-end carpentry with an immersive digital redesign
jikowood.role: Lead frontend ownership: architecture, gallery system, motion direction, localization, and quality through E2E coverage.

meta.title: Jikowood - Case study
meta.description: Premium redesign for a craft business with immersive gallery, Lenis, tests, and explicit conventions.

paragraphs: A premium craft business needs a digital presence that reflects the quality of its physical output, not a generic catalog-style website.
Project browsing has to feel fluid and memorable on mobile while staying respectful of performance constraints.
Project data is maintained as typed static content: five major projects and around forty visual assets.
A fullscreen gallery combines crossfade transitions with pure previous/next computations to keep navigation deterministic.

context.title: Context
paragraphs: The redesign (jikowood_refonte) is a full reset backed by ARCHITECTURE-CONVENTIONS and ROADMAP documents.
The contact flow UI is complete with Zod validation, while the final server-side delivery bridge remains to be connected.
bullets: Scope balances craft ambition and delivery realism
Conventions are written to preserve long-term coherence
Open unfinished points are explicit, not hidden

architecture.title: Architecture
paragraphs: The gallery lives in a dedicated `features/gallery` domain with its own README and dynamic `[slug]/[projectSlug]` route strategy.
Navigation logic is extracted into `lib/gallery/compute-*` modules so behavior stays side-effect free and testable.
bullets: Animated notch hero supported by motion-specific tests
Localization is shipped in fr / en / nl
Vitest and Playwright provide combined unit and E2E confidence

decisions.title: Decisions
paragraphs: Lenis is used for premium scrolling while reduced-motion fallback keeps the interaction accessible.
No CMS is introduced at this stage to preserve full control over visual consistency and pacing.
bullets: Motion choices are deliberate, not decorative
Accessibility fallback is treated as a core requirement
Content control remains local for quality assurance

execution.title: Execution
paragraphs: This project is strong proof of frontend craftsmanship combined with testing discipline on a premium showcase context.
Most photo assets are already integrated; production-grade captures are the main missing piece for final portfolio packaging.
bullets: Technical quality is visible in both UX and code structure
Remaining gap is mainly presentation assets
Delivery trajectory is clear and realistic

portfolio-pro.name: Portfolio Pro
portfolio-pro.tagline: The product system behind this website
portfolio-pro.role: Solo ownership across product strategy, architecture, motion system design, localization model, and design system governance.

meta.title: Portfolio Pro - Case study
meta.description: Building a premium portfolio as a real product shell, with motion architecture and documented strategy.

paragraphs: A generic portfolio cannot prove product thinking, architecture depth, or the ability to ship a coherent end-to-end shell.
The target was a premium, mobile-first experience with a clear identity, without copying standard Awwwards-style templates.
The delivery started with a documented multi-phase strategy before writing content: identity, UX logic, motion language, and architecture.
GSAP is modularized with one animation function per file, centralized timing control, and reduced-motion support throughout.

context.title: Intent
paragraphs: The positioning is frontend-first and product-oriented, with fullstack choices made where they reinforce product outcomes.
Phase 1 is shipped: splash, hero, profile manifesto, and animated navigation; project and contact layers are still in active build.
bullets: Positioning is explicit and technically backed
Shipping is phased to preserve quality under scope pressure
Narrative structure is designed, not improvised

architecture.title: Architecture
paragraphs: The architecture separates `features/` for screens, `components/` for reusable UI, and `lib/animation/` for motion infrastructure across 41 modules.
Localization relies on cookie-based i18n without `/fr` URL prefixes, keeping public URLs stable while locale selection stays internal.
bullets: Theme system interpolates 14 CSS variables through GSAP
Desktop leading/trailing navigation closes before route transition
Strict ESLint rules enforce no `any` and constrained `unknown` usage

decisions.title: Decisions
paragraphs: Framer Motion is intentionally excluded to keep GSAP behavior fully consistent across the shell.
The profile is structured in six product sections rather than a classic HTML biography block.
bullets: Brand-aligned favicon pipeline is generated through next/og
CSS tokens are enforced to avoid ad hoc color decisions
Project inventory is treated as the single source of truth

execution.title: Next steps
paragraphs: The next milestone is integrating complete case studies with media assets such as screenshots and videos.
Vercel deployment and real contact flow are the final execution proof, confirming this shell as a shipped product, not just a draft.
bullets: Case-study assets are the key remaining content dependency
Deployment is part of product validation
Execution quality is measured by shipped behavior

paragraphs: I build modern web applications with a product-oriented frontend approach.
I mainly work on SaaS, B2B, and internal tools where frontend execution directly shapes day-to-day use.

productMindset.title: Product mindset
intro: Understand the real need before the technical solution. Decide early, ship focused MVPs, keep a clear trajectory.
No superfluous features: a foundation that can grow without over-complicating too soon.
productMindset.outro: Every decision should answer a concrete use case, not a feature checklist.

frontendPhilosophy.title: Frontend philosophy
intro: Frontend sets a product’s rhythm, fluidity, and perception — not just the screen.
bullets: natural interactions
mobile comfort and smooth flows
visual consistency
details that make usage obvious
frontendPhilosophy.outro: Interfaces that read quickly and move without friction.

buildingApplications.title: What I build
intro: Tools built for real contexts: business workflows, daily use, product logic behind the screen.
bullets: SaaS platforms
business tools
dashboards
internal systems
applications with dense product logic
buildingApplications.outro: Real-world usage guides what we ship, not the other way around.

technicalApproach.title: Technical approach
intro: Simple, component-oriented architectures with coherent UI systems.
bullets: design systems
readable code
scalable foundations
clear separation of concerns
technicalApproach.outro: Technology should serve the product, not the other way around.

motionInteraction.title: Motion & interaction
intro: Animation and micro-interactions for hierarchy and fluidity — never for decoration.
motionInteraction.outro: Discreet, useful motion aligned with the rest of the interface.

collaborationExecution.title: Collaboration & execution
intro: Clear communication, sharp priorities, concrete progress.
bullets: grasp challenges quickly
useful decisions
clean delivery
autonomy without losing structure
collaborationExecution.outro: Serious products, well scoped, shipped cleanly.

closing.label: In short
paragraphs: SaaS, B2B, and modern tools with real product thinking.
Products built to last, evolve, and stay simple to use every day.

meta.title: Profile
meta.description: Product approach, frontend philosophy, and execution — Estime Vangu, product-oriented frontend engineer.
